Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple The J-magic Campaign targeted transportation systems by exploiting vulnerable Juniper routers to disrupt critical infrastructure operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2015-2424 [CWE-20]: Junos OS HTTP API allows remote code execution
stage.description: Attackers exploited a known vulnerability (CVE-2015-2424) in Juniper routers' HTTP API, enabling remote code execution on internet-facing devices to gain initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-30807 [CWE-20]: Junos OS allows arbitrary command execution via crafted packets
stage.description: Attackers deployed the J-magic backdoor using command-line interpreters on compromised routers, establishing persistent control over the device.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Attackers manipulated routing configurations or triggered denial-of-service conditions to disrupt transportation systems like traffic lights and train signals.

Scenario.summary: The J-magic Campaign exploited unpatched Juniper routers via remote code execution vulnerabilities, deploying backdoors to disrupt transportation infrastructure operations.
